name: Egide Abahuje
focus_areas: Dr. Abahuje’s current global health interests include enhancing patient safety by improving perioperative teams’ non-technical skills (situational awareness, decision-making, leadership, communication, and teamwork). He is also interested in advancing the quality of health-professional education by conducting medical-education capacity-building training for health professionals in LMICs. Furthermore, he is interested in developing multidisciplinary, collaborative quality-improvement initiatives that leverage data to advance patient-safety practice.

name: Zhong Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Wang collaborates globally to develop stem-cell–based heart therapies to prolong and improve the lives of cardiac patients. His lab applies cutting-edge technologies—including stem cell biology, tissue engineering, and genetically modified large-animal models—to help achieve this goal.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Middle East & North Africa

name: Dawen Cai
focus_areas: Dr. Cai is interested in developing novel genetic labeling, imaging, and single-cell profiling techniques and applying them to study diseases related to neurodevelopmental disorders, neurodegeneration, and cancer.
countries_normalized: China, Germany, United Kingdom, Japan, Vietnam

name: Brian Athey
professional_title: Michael Savageau Collegiate Professor & Chair of Computational Medicine & Bioinformatics
focus_areas: Dr. Athey's research interests center on creating and using AI/ML methods, coupled with long-read sequencing and novel pharmacometabolomics technologies, to radically improve pharmacogenomics—so patients receive the most effective drugs with the fewest side effects. Current projects include: (1) collaborating with the Rogel Cancer Center Joint Institute and Dr. Balram Chowbay at Singapore National Cancer Centre to reanalyze breast-cancer patient data using long-read sequencing; (2) working with Dr. Toshihiro Ito and leaders at Nara Medical University to help guide the formation of a new Department of Computational Medicine, deliver a keynote on AI and Medicine for NMU’s 80th Anniversary, and lead faculty exchanges between U-M Medical School and NMU (launched in 2018), including a planned visit in conjunction with the 2026 UM Pan Asia Conference in Tokyo; and (3) collaborating through the Michigan Medicine–Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute with Dr. Lin Lu’s laboratory to validate a new pharmacogenomic/metabolomic diagnostic assay system for Bipolar Disorder-1 medication selection in a Chinese population, with visiting scholar Dr. Jie Sun working with his group.

name: Margit Burmeister
focus_areas: Dr. Burmeister has active research collaborations in (epi)genetics and big data in China (Shanghai Jiao Tong University, PUHSC) and in rare-disease genetics in Turkey. She also facilitates student and faculty exchanges with, and teaches in, China.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Europe, Middle East & North Africa

name: Brent Ward
focus_areas: Dr. Ward focuses on building collaborative research in head and neck cancer, with particular emphasis on the oral cavity and microvascular reconstruction. He brings expertise spanning bench-top discovery in drug delivery, database development, and clinical trial design.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia

name: Trilokraj Tejasvi
focus_areas: Dr. Tejasvi focuses on providing health services and medical education in underserved areas through teledermatology; building awareness and providing medical education for residents and medical students in tropical dermatology via externships; and studying the genetic basis of common dermatologic diseases across different ethnicities.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia

name: Alex Tsoi
focus_areas: Dr. Tsoi investigates the pathology and genetic architecture of complex cutaneous disorders using systems-biology approaches. His collaboration through the Michigan Medicine–Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute explores the clinical and molecular determinants of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma and represents the first dermatology-focused collaboration between the two institutions.

name: Robert Neumar
focus_areas: Dr. Neumar is interested in facilitating global research collaborations in emergency medicine and critical care.
countries_normalized: Ghana, Taiwan, United Kingdom, Singapore, Australia, Canada
regions_derived: Sub-Saharan Africa, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Europe, North America

name: Rockefeller Oteng
focus_areas: Dr. Oteng’s work primarily focuses on creating human capacity for health through the development of sustainable training programs and their evaluation.

name: Vijay Singh
focus_areas: Dr. Singh’s research focuses on intimate partner violence identification and response in health-care settings. He is interested in training health-care providers in LMICs to address violence against women.

name: J. Scott Van Epps
focus_areas: Dr. Van Epps' research is currently focused on life threatening infections, including his collaboration through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute which is exploring the use of a novel rapid PCR assay to detect bacteria in whole blood without culture using ultrasensitive nanorod technology.

name: Juana Nicole Capizzano
focus_areas: Dr. Capizzano is interested in teaching point-of-care ultrasound and expanding access to ultrasound in underserved communities.

name: Katherine Gold
focus_areas: Dr. Gold focuses on maternal-child health, particularly in the perinatal period. She is interested in stillbirth and infant-death prevention, as well as improving maternal mental health.

name: Timothy Guetterman
focus_areas: Dr. Guetterman’s global health interests include research capacity building—particularly quantitative, qualitative, and mixed-methods skills—with extensive work in South Africa and other nations. He also focuses on culturally adapting cognitive-ability tests for children with disabilities and examining factors related to outcomes in the Republic of Georgia. In addition to his mixed-methods scholarship, he researches the use of informatics and telehealth technologies in health care and examines the safety and quality of cardiovascular care.
regions_derived: Sub-Saharan Africa, Middle East & North Africa

name: Diane Harper
focus_areas: Dr. Harper focuses on cervical cancer screening methods and triage follow-up.
regions_derived: North America, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Sahoko Little
focus_areas: Dr. Little is the clinical director of the Japanese Family Health Program, providing culturally and linguistically competent care to the Japanese population in Southeast Michigan. Her program also hosts exchange students and residents between the U.S. and Japan.

name: Elham Mahmoudi
focus_areas: Dr. Mahmoudi’s research centers on improving dementia care in the United States, investigating health-care utilization, costs, system efficiency, and equity for older adults with Alzheimer’s disease, related dementias, or cognitive decline. Her work includes international collaboration—particularly with partners in China—and emphasizes cross-country comparisons to identify best practices and inform global strategies for dementia care and healthy aging.
countries_normalized: United Kingdom, France, Germany, China, Japan

name: Sherri Sheinfeld-Gorin
focus_areas: Dr. Sheinfeld-Gorin’s recent global health work focuses on decreasing HPV-vaccine hesitancy via policy and intervention programs in LMICs. She spent Spring 2023 on a Fulbright Scholarship in Israel collaborating on this issue.
countries_normalized: Colombia, Uganda, Israel, Democratic Republic of the Congo
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Sub-Saharan Africa, Middle East & North Africa

name: Stephanie Bielas
professional_title: Morton S and Henrietta K Sellner Professor of Human Genetics, Associate Professor of Human Genetics
focus_areas: Dr. Bielas’s international collaborations focus on pediatric medical-genetics projects, including genetic diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders and functional allele testing. Her team has worked to bridge gaps in the availability of next-generation sequencing technology in medical-genetics settings in India.

name: David Burke
focus_areas: Dr. Burke develops health technologies for remote and underserved communities, with a primary focus on low-capital-cost digital health-monitoring systems and a secondary focus on locally fabricable technologies such as 3D-printed applications.

name: Sally Camper
professional_title: Margery Shaw Distinguished University Professor of Human Genetics
focus_areas: Dr. Camper collaborates with experts at other institutions around the world to study rare diseases, particularly disorders of craniofacial development that affect pituitary function and/or hearing.
countries_normalized: Argentina, Brazil, United Kingdom, France, Japan
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Europe, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Thomas Glover
focus_areas: Dr. Glover aims to advance the knowledge and application of human genetics in Ghana through lectures, meetings with faculty, students, and physicians, and advising colleagues at the University of Accra. His work provides guidance on educational, research, and diagnostic fronts while also informing his understanding of broader needs and opportunities for genetic research, education, and diagnostics in West Africa.

name: Kalpesh Unadkat
focus_areas: Dr. Unadkat focuses on enabling the use of information technology to facilitate the collection, consolidation, and analysis of health information. His work aims to improve efficiency in research management and support clinicians in organizing, extracting, and using data for trending, reporting, and decision-making.

name: Celine Berthier
focus_areas: Dr. Berthier’s work focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of IgA nephropathy (IgAN), and lupus/lupus nephritis (LN). She has research collaborations through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute.

name: J. Brian Byrd
focus_areas: Dr. Byrd’s laboratory works to improve the health of patients with high blood pressure—the leading global risk factor for disease burden. With over one billion people affected worldwide, he collaborates with international partners to improve blood-pressure control for patients wherever they live.

name: Lizbeth Cahuayme-Zuniga
focus_areas: Dr. Cahuayme-Zuniga’s research encompasses infectious diseases in immunocompromised patients, particularly solid-organ transplant recipients. She is interested in establishing robust mentorship and leadership programs to empower medical trainees and junior faculty in low-resource settings.

name: Tae-Hwa Chun
focus_areas: Dr. Chun works to understand the pathogenesis of metabolic and endocrine disorders, particularly obesity and diabetes.

name: Thomas Crawford
focus_areas: Dr. Crawford oversees the My Heart Your Heart program, a cardiac pacemaker-reuse initiative at the Cardiovascular Center. His team evaluates pacemaker reuse, including safety, efficacy, and the associated ethical and regulatory considerations.
countries_normalized: Bolivia, Ghana, Kenya, Mexico, Pakistan, Philippines, Uganda
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Daniel Eitzman
focus_areas: Dr. Eitzman’s research focuses on the genetic determinants of thrombosis, atherosclerosis, and inflammation. His research in collaboration through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute explores how metabolic variations impact the effectiveness of antiplatelet therapies.

name: David Flood
focus_areas: Dr. Flood carries out direct service, health-worker teaching, and health-services research in Indigenous communities in Guatemala, with projects focused on noncommunicable diseases in children and adults.

name: Jennifer Griggs
focus_areas: Dr. Griggs is a volunteer Physician for Asylum Seekers' Network of Physicians for Human Rights; addressing the problem of breast cancer in Haiti, one of the poorest countries in the world.

name: Gary Hammer
professional_title: Mille Schembechler Professor of Adrenal Cancer, Molecular & Integrative Physiology/Internal Medicine
focus_areas: Dr. Hammer focuses on adrenal and other endocrinological cancers.

name: Michele Heisler
focus_areas: Development and evaluation of interventions to improve patient chronic disease self-management and outcomes in low-resource settings; longitudinal, population-based research to inform better targeting and implementation of primary care programs.

name: William Herman
professional_title: Stefan S Fajans M.D./GlaxoSmithKline Professor of Diabetes
focus_areas: Dr. Herman leads international capacity-building to address the rising global prevalence of diabetes, including efforts to (1) better characterize the diabetes population in Sri Lanka, (2) devise resource-conscious screening and monitoring methods, and (3) expand the pool of ancillary care providers to relieve health-system congestion from physician shortages.

name: Timothy Hofer
focus_areas: Dr. Hofer studies methods to measure and improve health and health-care quality, with an emphasis on informal urban settlements in low- and middle-income countries.
countries_normalized: Nigeria, Kenya, Bangladesh, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Malawi

name: Joel Howell
professional_title: Elizabeth Farrand Collegiate Professor of Medical History
focus_areas: Dr. Howell collaborates with colleagues in Brazil on (a) the comparative introduction and use of medical technology and (b) the politics of international medical collaboration during wartime.

name: Amy Huang
focus_areas: Dr. Huang is the Global REACH Director for Asia Programs as well as the administative lead for the Michigan Medicine-PKUHSC Joint Institute, a research collaboration between UMMS and Peking University Health Science Center.

name: Robert Hyzy
focus_areas: Dr. Hyzy has engaged in providing medical outreach to the indigent, under served population of the Peruvian Amazon River basin as the Medical Director of Amazon Promise, as US registered NGO based in Iquitos, Peru. Additionally, to provide an educational opportunity to University of Michigan Medical students and residents during an annual M-4 elective rotation.

name: Wenjun Ju
focus_areas: Dr. Ju is an executive member of the Michigan Medicine–Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute (JI), has helped lead JI renal research programs, and serves on the advisory committee of the Women’s Leadership Fellowship program.
countries_normalized: China, Italy, Spain, Sweden, The Netherlands

name: Venkat Keshamouni
focus_areas: Dr. Keshamouni is interested in deciphering cellular and molecular interactions in the tumor microenvironment (TME) that contribute to tumor growth and metastasis. His collaboration(link is external) through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute explores therapeutic implications of natural killer cell immune surveillance in lung cancer.

name: Joseph Kolars
focus_areas: Dr. Kolars has served at U-M since 2009 and has received numerous education awards, including the 2019 AAMC Abraham Flexner Award for Distinguished Service to Medical Education. He has served as UMMS Senior Associate Dean for Education and Global Initiatives and as program director for Internal Medicine residency and fellowship programs at U-M and at Mayo Clinic. His team is among the leaders in the AMA’s Transforming Medical Education initiative. He is a member of the Fogarty International Center Advisory Council and co-PI on the Northern Pacific Global Health Research Fellows Training Consortium.
countries_normalized: Brazil, China, Ethiopia, Ghana, India, Nepal, Peru, Tanzania, Uganda, Vietnam, Zimbabwe
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia

name: Matthias Kretzler
focus_areas: Dr. Kretzler builds research networks for translational nephrology across five continents, integrating comprehensive clinical datasets with large-scale genetic and genomic information.
countries_normalized: Nigeria, Ghana, Norway, United Kingdom, Sweden, Denmark, China, Germany, Switzerland, Italy, France, Japan
regions_derived: Sub-Saharan Africa, Europe, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Stanley Kuo
focus_areas: Dr. Kuo uses health economics and outcomes research, simulation modeling, decision-analytic science, and health-services research to inform clinical care and health-policy decision-making for diabetes management.

name: Qing LI
focus_areas: Dr. Li’s research focuses on how oncogenic mutations dysregulate cytokine signaling, cell-stress-response signaling, and cellular metabolism in hematopoietic cells. She co-leads a Michigan Medicine–PUHSC Joint Institute collaboration exploring the role of ER-associated degradation (ERAD) in antigen presentation and immune evasion in multiple myeloma.

name: Anna Suk-Fong Lok
focus_areas: Dr. Lok’s current collaborations include: (1) the Chinese University of Hong Kong (PI: Dr. Grace Wong) on cost-effectiveness modeling for HBV cure therapies; and (2) Chang Gung Medical College in Taiwan (PI: Dr. Rachel WJ Jeng) on the risk of HBV reactivation associated with commonly used immunosuppressive therapies.

name: Marisa H. Miceli
focus_areas: Dr. Miceli focuses on the global impact of transplant infectious diseases and invasive fungal infections, with the goal of improving outcomes and quality of life for immunocompromised individuals. She aims to advance global research collaborations and clinical practices that support innovative approaches to infection prevention, diagnosis, and treatment in low-resource settings.

name: Victor Moles
focus_areas: Dr. Moles is interested in building long-term partnerships with Latin America to improve health-care delivery through cultural, clinical, and research exchange. His areas of interest include quality improvement, cardiovascular-disease prevention, and pulmonary hypertension.

name: Marcos Montagnini
focus_areas: Dr. Montagnini’s interests include geriatrics, palliative medicine, and rehabilitation medicine for older adults and people with cancer. He seeks partnerships focused on medical and interprofessional education, innovative care models, clinical research, and quality assurance.

name: Andre Monteiro da Rocha
focus_areas: Dr. Monteiro da Rocha’s research focuses on aging of cardiac muscle, with a growing interest in skeletal-muscle aging. His laboratory also focus on developing personalized pahrmacotherapy tools.

name: Nithya Ramnath
focus_areas: Dr. Ramnath focuses on solid-organ cancer therapeutics, precision oncology, and lung-cancer screening, with collaborations including Christian Medical College–Vellore, AIIMS Jammu, and JIPMER Pondicherry.

name: James Riddell
focus_areas: Dr. Riddell develops programs to improve HIV outcomes in resource-poor settings. Working with Dean Yang (Ford School of Public Policy), he has studied interventions to improve ART adherence—finding combined economic incentives and phone reminders most effective—and is currently evaluating the use of “health promoters” in rural Mozambique, including optimal hiring approaches and impact on HIV testing and adherence.

name: Sanjay Saint
focus_areas: Dr. Saint's research explores the prevention of healthcare-associated infections as well as hand hygiene: projects aimed at assessing and increasing hand hygiene compliance among healthcare workers and preventing common healthcare-associated infections such as catheter associated urinary tract infection (CAUTI), central line-associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI), and ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP). Also interested in leadership, followership, mindfulness, and implementation science.
countries_normalized: Italy, Japan, Thailand, Australia, New Zealand, Switzerland

name: Elizabeth Speliotes
focus_areas: Dr. Speliotes focuses on nonalcoholic fatty liver disease, obesity and related metabolic diseases. This includes her collaboration(link is external) through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute, which explores genetic and microbiome effects on non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and its medical complications in a Chinese population.

name: Scott Stonington
focus_areas: Dr. Stonington studies the globalization of biomedicine as a system of ethics, power, and worldview, with fieldwork on transformations in end-of-life care and chronic-pain management in Thailand and the U.S. His new projects focus on international global-mental-health interventions in Peru.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Latin America & Caribbean

name: Porama Thanaporn
focus_areas: Dr. Thanaporn’s primary interest is the treatment of cardiovascular disease, particularly heart failure and hypertension, and addressing rising cardiovascular risk in resource-limited settings. He aims to identify locally feasible strategies using precision medicine and leveraging both new pharmaceuticals and repurposed generics.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia

name: John Varga
focus_areas: Dr. Varga’s research focuses on understanding the genetic and environmental differences in scleroderma between populations in China and the US. How do genetic factors between these populations account for differences in susceptibility, severity, progression and responses to therapy? How do these insights apply globally, and what impact they have on early diagnosis, treatment, and prevention?
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia, Latin America & Caribbean

name: Thomas Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Wang's primary focus in global health is to develop novel methodologies for early detection, staging, and image-guided therapy of cancers with worldwide prevalence.

name: Max Wicha
focus_areas: Dr. Wicha’s career has focused on the biology and treatment of breast cancer and he has been a major leader in the science of cancer stem cells. He co-leads a collaboration through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute exploring the role of exosome signaling in cancer cell dedifferentiation and chemo-resistance.

name: Zach Landis-Lewis
focus_areas: Dr. Landis-Lewis is interested in the use of medical record data in district hospitals in Malawi to generate tailored performance feedback for healthcare providers. I am studying knowledge representation methods for theory-informed message tailoring systems that can be used in low-resource settings

name: Cheryl Moyer
focus_areas: Dr. Moyer focuses on maternal and neonatal health in sub-Saharan Africa, emphasizing social and cultural factors that affect clinical outcomes. Current projects address neonatal jaundice screening and diagnosis, home-based blood-pressure monitoring for pregnant women, and postpartum family-planning decision-making. She also serves as Michigan PI for the Northern Pacific Global Health consortium, which offers in-country pre- and post-doctoral training fellowships.

name: Deborah Rooney
focus_areas: Dr. Rooney's research interests include surgical education, simulation-based education and assessment.
regions_derived: Sub-Saharan Africa, Middle East & North Africa

name: Akbar Waljee
focus_areas: Dr. Waljee works to establish links among academic organizations, hospitals, and universities in developed and developing countries—particularly in East Africa—to address disparities in surgical and medical care, currently through the RJW Foundation.

name: Cheong-Hee Chang
focus_areas: Dr. Chang studies factors regulating T-cell–mediated immune responses, with particular interest in cell metabolism and mechanisms influencing T-cell survival and function. Additional work examines the effects of indigenous plant extracts on T-cell behavior relevant to malaria prevention and the impact of iron on T-cell function. A collaboration with MUST (Uganda) ended in 2024 without a manuscript; she remains enthusiastic about teaching immunology at MUST or elsewhere in Africa.

name: Yongqun He
focus_areas: Dr. He focuses on biomedical ontology, vaccine informatics, nephrology, infectious diseases, and big-data representation/analysis. His team develops and applies biomedical ontologies to support integrative knowledge representation and “AI-ready” data, and he is interested in machine-learning methods for vaccine design and precision medicine.

name: Akira Ono
focus_areas: Dr. Ono investigates molecular mechanisms by which HIV-1 and influenza A virus assemble progeny viruses and spread to uninfected cells, aiming to develop therapeutics targeting these pathogens. His lab also hosts—and is willing to continue hosting—virology trainees from resource-limited countries to share research techniques.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Malini Raghavan
focus_areas: Dr. Raghavan’s research examines how polymorphisms in immune-response genes (major histocompatibility complex class I) influence immunity to infections and vaccine outcomes, particularly in the context of HIV. She is also interested in contributing to vaccine-distribution efforts and expanding bone-marrow registries in India.

name: Alice Telesnitsky
focus_areas: Dr. Telesnitsky collaborates with students and faculty at the West African Centre for Cell Biology of Infectious Pathogens (WACCBIP), University of Ghana, to study how HIV-1 gene expression varies among infected cells and how this variation affects persistence during antiretroviral therapy.

name: Jun Hee Lee
focus_areas: Dr. Lee focuses on the relationship between stress, aging, and metabolism, including stress-inducible Sestrins and their role in age- and obesity-associated metabolic pathologies, as well as mechanisms underlying autophagy disruption in human disease (including nonalcoholic fatty liver disease). His team develops and applies advanced single-cell and spatial omics technologies to investigate cellular stress responses and translate insights into therapeutic strategies.

name: Costas Lyssiotis
focus_areas: Dr. Lyssiotis uses a multi-disciplinary approach encompassing methods in chemistry and biology to define how metabolism is rewired in cancer. He leads a collaboration(link is external) through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute to define the role of malic enzymes in gastric cancers. The malic enzyme project with JI has led to an extension of another active internation collaboration with the Sadanandam lab in the United Kingdom. Dr. Lyssiotis leads a project identifying metabolic vulnerabilities of pancreatic cancer for potential therapeutic strategies. He leads a collaboration with Dr. Sadanandam at the Institute of Cancer Research in the UK to identify the necessity for uridine in pancreatic tumor progression using multi-omics approaches.

name: William Rainey
focus_areas: Dr. Rainey studies regulation of adrenal steroid production and diseases caused by steroid excess (e.g., hypertension, cardiovascular disease, obesity, infertility). His team participates in multicenter studies with U.S. and international universities and hospitals that have adrenal-disease referral programs.
countries_normalized: Australia, South Korea, Spain, Germany, South Africa
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Europe, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Carol Elias
focus_areas: Dr. Elias investigates mechanisms by which metabolic imbalance disrupts reproductive function, with special attention to the neural and molecular basis of leptin action in the neuroendocrine reproductive axis.

name: Melissa Elafros
focus_areas: Dr. Elafros leads the Z-CHAD (Zambian Cohort of Healthy Aging and Dementia) Study, a population-based study of cognitive impairment among adults >55 in Lusaka. Her team evaluates dementia prevalence, risk factors, and understanding among adults, caregivers, and providers, aiming to improve care and quality of life for people with dementia in Zambia.

name: Eva Feldman
professional_title: James W Albers Distinguished University Professor of Neurology, Russell N DeJong Professor of Neurology
focus_areas: Dr. Feldman studies mechanisms of neurodegeneration and develops treatments for diabetic neuropathy, ALS, and Alzheimer’s disease. A major international effort evaluates metabolic factors associated with diabetic neuropathy across diverse populations to identify shared and distinct features that illuminate mechanisms and potential therapeutic targets.
regions_derived: South Asia, Europe, Middle East & North Africa

name: Amro M. Stino
focus_areas: Dr. Amro Maher Stino is interested in peripheral neuropathy research collaborations and provider education.

name: Venkatakrishna Rajajee
focus_areas: Dr. Rajajee focuses on the care of patients with traumatic brain injury in the developing world and on the management of acute brain injury (stroke, seizures, hemorrhage).

name: Yamaan Saadeh
focus_areas: Dr. Saadeh is focused improving access to high quality subspeciality neurosurgical and peripheral nerve training in areas of need around the world.

name: Emma Lawrence
focus_areas: Dr. Lawrence studies disparities and drivers of global maternal morbidity and mortality; evaluates capacity-building and interventions to address those drivers; and assesses global medical-education initiatives, including cross-cultural relationships in curriculum development and training.

name: Dhanu Thiyagarajan
focus_areas: Dr. Thiyagarajan designs and clinically evaluates medical devices and simulation-based curricula for women’s health tailored to low-resource settings. Recent device projects include urine-based HPV screening, a uterine blood-loss quantification/diagnosis device, and a mechanical rate-controlled IV infusion pump; recent simulation projects include curricula for external cephalic version, hysteroscopy, and gynecologic laparoscopy.

name: Sarah Compton
focus_areas: Dr. Compton focuses on contraception and family planning in Ghana, as well as the prevention of sexual and gender-based violence.

name: John DeLancey
professional_title: Norman F Miller Professor of Gynecology
focus_areas: Dr. DeLancey specializes in urogynecology and pelvic-floor disorders and maintains research collaborations through the Michigan Medicine–PUHSC Joint Institute. His lab has hosted visiting scholars and researchers from around the world, including Brazil, Italy, Switzerland, Iran, Qatar, and elsewhere.

name: Edward Goldman
focus_areas: Dr. Goldman collaborates with law faculty in Kumasi, Ghana, to create a master’s program in reproductive justice.

name: Bernadete Ayres
professional_title: Assitant Professor of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
focus_areas: Dr. Ayres focuses on mutually beneficial research and education in ophthalmic ultrasound and has worked with colleagues to establish an ophthalmology residency program at St. Paul’s Hospital Millennium Medical College in Ethiopia.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Sub-Saharan Africa, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Wayne Cornblath
focus_areas: Promoting neuroophthalmolgy training/teaching on a global level to increase access and quality of care.
regions_derived: Middle East & North Africa, Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Sherry Day
focus_areas: Dr. Day supports overseas training for physicians in specialty contact-lens fitting (for diseased or post-op corneas) and low-vision rehabilitation, with backing from international ophthalmology leadership. She and her Kellogg Eye Center colleagues have hosted trainees from around the world, most recently Pakistan.

name: Karen Deloss
focus_areas: Dr. Deloss' primary interests include developing mutually beneficial research and educational collaborations in the areas of ocular surface disease as well as disorders of the cornea including keratoconus and corneal ectasia.

name: Hakan Demirci
professional_title: Richard N. and Marilyn K. Witham Professor of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
focus_areas: Dr. Demirci's research primarily focuses on ocular oncology including intraocular, conjunctival, orbital and eyelid tumors.

name: Joshua Ehrlich
focus_areas: Dr. Ehrlich focuses on research collaborations and capacity strengthening, working with global partners to study population aging and the consequences of visual and multisensory impairment on health and well-being in older adults. He is invested in strengthening research capacity to improve health outcomes globally.

name: Patrice Fort
focus_areas: Dr. Fort’s work focuses on mechanisms that protect the retina and how these pathways are altered in disorders such as diabetic retinopathy, age-related macular degeneration, retinal detachment, and glaucoma. His team participates in collaborative research to identify ways to use, enhance, or restore these protective pathways to develop new treatment options for retinal neurodegeneration.

name: Christopher Thomas Hood
focus_areas: Dr. Hood has been involved in ongoing medical medical missions and volunteerism in the Caribbean.

name: Otana Jakpor
focus_areas: Dr. Jakpor aims to improve access to high-quality eye care for children worldwide, with a particular interest in global ophthalmology educational partnerships.

name: Zvi Kresch
focus_areas: Dr. Kresch spent extended periods abroad teaching and practicing medicine prior to joining the University of Michigan and has continued this work through projects with partners in Ethiopia and Peru.

name: Paul Lee
focus_areas: Dr. Lee focuses on glaucoma, cataract, and anterior segment disease.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia

name: Helios Leung
focus_areas: Optometric education

name: Shahzad Mian
focus_areas: Dr. Mian works to expand capacity for corneal transplants in developing countries, reduce adverse events associated with transplants, and improve management of pediatric corneal disease.

name: Christine Nelson
focus_areas: Dr. Nelson explores collaborations in education and telemedicine in oculoplastic and orbital surgery; teaches surgical techniques to residents and local ophthalmologists; and conducts clinical research in congenital eye disease (including ptosis and anophthalmia genetics) and in orbital/skin cancer to improve quality of life and surgical techniques.
countries_normalized: Ethiopia, Saudi Arabia, Nepal, Brazil, Kenya, Jamaica
regions_derived: Sub-Saharan Africa, Middle East & North Africa, South Asia, Latin America & Caribbean

name: Paula Anne Newman-Casey
focus_areas: Dr. Newman-Casey's work focuses on decreasing rates of blindness through creating systems-based approaches to improving access to care; creating effective educational curricula for para-medical personnel; and studying patient decision making and health behavior change.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, South Asia, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Lev Prasov
focus_areas: Dr. Prasov is an ophthalmologist and ophthalmic geneticist studying pediatric glaucoma and nanophthalmos, aiming to improve visual outcomes worldwide. His primary collaborations are with the University of São Paulo, Brazil, to define genetic heterogeneity and novel variants causing nanophthalmos and extreme hyperopia and to help establish genetic-testing pipelines for other ocular genetic disorders.

name: Rajesh Rao
focus_areas: Dr. Rao leads a first-in-human, NIH-funded adult stem-cell trial for AMD; pioneers liquid biopsies for intraocular lymphoma detection and diabetic retinopathy; and studies epigenetic regulation of retinal development and disease (AMD, diabetic retinopathy, rare developmental disorders) to identify biomarkers, therapeutic targets, and stem-cell clinical applications.

name: Kaz Soong
focus_areas: Dr. Soong has engaged in many International ophthalmology collaborations over the years. Most recently, he has worked with partners in Ukraine to help establish the nation's first eye bank and other infrastructure to support corneal transplant procedures.
countries_normalized: Brazil, Bulgaria, China, Haiti, India, Japan, Taiwan
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Europe, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia

name: Joshua D. Stein
focus_areas: Dr. Stein's work focuses on utilization of eye care services and outcomes of eye care in developing nations and how they compare with the US, as well as alternative health care delivery models for eye care in other countries.

name: Grace Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Wang partners with colleagues in Ghana to advise pediatric ophthalmology fellows in a jointly designed post-residency program. She is interested in expanding global medical education, exchanging knowledge and technology, and partnering to conduct clinical research in pediatric ophthalmology and adult strabismus.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Maria A. Woodward
focus_areas: Dr. Woodward explores new and emerging telemedicine for eye diseases in LMIC settings.

name: David Zacks
focus_areas: Dr. Zacks is focused on understanding the mechanisms underlying retinal cell death and degeneration during retinal disease. He has an active research collaboration with colleagues in China through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute.

name: Glenn Green
focus_areas: Dr. Green focuses on translational interventions for complex communication disorders affecting speech and hearing.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Europe, Middle East & North Africa

name: Jeffrey Moyer
focus_areas: Dr. Moyer focuses on capacity development in otolaryngology in sub-Saharan Africa, particularly Ghana.

name: Mark Prince
focus_areas: Dr. Prince works to increase patient-care and research capacity through medical-student and postgraduate medical-education programs.

name: Rouba Ali-Fehmi
focus_areas: Dr. Ali-Fehmi, immediate past-president of NAAMA and founder/chair of NAAMA NextGen, focuses on bi-directional training for pre-medical and medical students and doctors in training, and advocates on public-health issues impacting refugee populations. Long-standing partnerships include the American University of Beirut, Amel (a health-care NGO in Lebanon), and King Hussein University in Jordan.

name: Arul Chinnaiyan
professional_title: S.P. Hicks Endowed Professor of Pathology
focus_areas: Dr. Chinnaiyan is interested in functional genomic, proteomic and bioinformatics approaches to study cancer and understand cancer biology, as well as the discovery of clinical biomarkers for cancer. His group has characterized a number of important biomarkers of prostate cancer. Dr. Chinnaiyan co-leads a project to establish a Cancer Biomarker Collaborative(link is external) through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute.

name: Chandan Kumar
focus_areas: Dr. Kumar seeks research, training, and educational collaborations in translational cancer genomics and related areas in India and other countries in the subcontinent.
regions_derived: South Asia, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Zaneta Nikolovska-Coleska
focus_areas: Dr. Nikolovska-Coleska’s research group focuses on chemical genomics and the discovery and development of active compounds to interrogate biological systems and improve human health. Her team studies protein–protein interactions and their roles in normal and disease states, with a focus on cancer, to develop novel therapeutic approaches and target-selective agents. She also aims to expand research and training capacity through junior-scientist exchanges and graduate and postgraduate education programs.

name: Lee Schroeder
focus_areas: Dr. Schroeder uses decision-analytic approaches to model and improve the impact of laboratory medicine on global health-care delivery, including laboratory-capacity landscape studies and modeling the effects of test strategy and quality on health outcomes.

name: Jiaqi Shi
focus_areas: Dr. Shi’s laboratory focuses on pancreatic cancer biology, currently investigating epigenetic regulation of the pancreatic tumor microenvironment.

name: Xiaoju Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Xiaoju Wang’s research focuses on immunomic and proteomic approaches to discover cancer biomarkers and to develop inhibitors as new molecularly targeted cancer therapies. She is particularly interested in targeting protein–protein interactions involved in transcriptional regulation, including identifying peptidomimetic inhibitors of the ERG transcription factor, an oncogenic driver in both hormone-naïve and castration-resistant prostate cancer.

name: David Bradley
focus_areas: Dr. Bradley, a pediatric cardiologist, collaborates internationally in cardiac rhythm care and capacity building, with primary partnerships in Rwanda and additional clinical collaborations in Gabon and Guatemala.

name: Rajen Mody
focus_areas: Dr. Mody seeks to improve outcomes of Pediatric Cancers in resource poor countries by: 1) Empowering Local Champions design and adopt clinical treatment protocols, which are feasible in their settings and in their patient population; and 2) Improving infrastructure for clinical research including creating database, improving nursing, pharmacy and other supportive care services.

name: Nate Nessle
focus_areas: Dr. Nessle's primary global health interests are concentrated on decreasing the burden of disease in children with cancer and sickle cell disease across sub-Saharan Africa. He conducts impactful clinical research studies using implementation science methodologies to improve cancer care delivery with a current focus on improving fever management. He is interested in novel uses of wearable sensors and artificial intelligence in global oncology and low-resourced settings

name: Mark Norris
focus_areas: Dr. Norris, a pediatric and adult congenital cardiologist, focuses on congenital and acquired heart disease from childhood through adulthood, including telemedicine echocardiography and clinical consultation, on-site surgical candidate screenings, perioperative support, and medical-team education

name: Vasantha Padmanabhan
focus_areas: Dr. Padmanabhan conducts translational research on the developmental origins of pubertal and adult reproductive and metabolic disorders. Studies examine how maternal exposure to native steroids (testosterone, estradiol) and endocrine-disrupting pollutants (e.g., BPA, phthalates) alters fetal development and programs adult disease—using approaches from cell/molecular biology and in vitro systems to whole-animal physiology and human translation—to illuminate mechanisms and identify prevention/treatment strategies, including for PCOS.

name: Shane Quinonez
focus_areas: Dr. Shane Quinonez is a Clinical Associate Professor in the Department of Pediatrics and Internal Medicine, specializing in Genetics, Metabolism, and Genomic Medicine. He earned his medical degree from the University of Michigan and then completed his residency in Pediatrics, followed by a residency in Clinical Genetics and a fellowship in Clinical Biochemical Genetics, all at the University of Michigan. In addition to his role as Interim Director of Global REACH, Dr. Quinonez is currently an Associate Program Director of the Pediatric Residency Program and Co-Director of the Global Health and Disparities Pathway in the medical school. Dr. Quinonez’s international efforts focus on the introduction of medical genetics services in low- and middle-income countries and include active projects in Ghana and Nepal. In addition to his clinical and educational roles, Dr. Quinonez co-directs the Michigan Medical Genetics Biochemical Laboratory and serves as the Service Chief of Pediatric Genetics, Metabolism, and Genomic Medicine. He is dedicated to reducing health disparities globally and mentoring medical students and house officers with this same interest.

name: Libak Abou
focus_areas: Fluent in French and Portuguese, Dr. Abou is committed to enhancing pain education and reducing disparities in physical activity in low-income settings. His areas of focus include neurological conditions such as stroke, spinal cord injury, and multiple sclerosis.

name: Emily Briceno
focus_areas: Dr. Briceno is interested in cognitive health disparities in aging populations and in the measurement of cognition across culturally and linguistically diverse older adult populations.

name: Chandramouli Krishnan
focus_areas: Dr. Krishnan’s Neuromuscular and Rehabilitation Robotics Laboratory (NeuRRo Lab) develops effective, efficient rehabilitation methods for individuals with neurological and orthopedic disorders, combining mechanistic and interventional approaches to advance understanding of neuromuscular function, movement control, and plasticity. He welcomes collaborative research and mentoring/training for international scholars and visiting scientists.
countries_normalized: India, Brazil, Australia, Israel, New Zealand
regions_derived: South Asia, Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Middle East & North Africa

name: Mark Peterson
focus_areas: Dr. Peterson’s global health work focuses on surveillance of secondary health conditions in pediatric neuromuscular disabilities (e.g., cerebral palsy) across the lifespan, as well as developing and refining behavioral interventions to reduce morbidity and sustain healthy life years in these populations. He is also interested in studying the causes and consequences of age-related frailty and cardiometabolic disorders, with emphasis on musculoskeletal pathophysiology and physical-activity epidemiology.
countries_normalized: Brazil, Ireland, Israel, China, Netherlands, Spain
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, Europe, Middle East & North Africa, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Gianna Rodriguez
focus_areas: Dr. Rodriguez focuses on disability and neurorehabilitation, including acute and chronic sequelae of spinal cord and brain injury and disease.

name: Sean Smith
focus_areas: Dr. Smith develops physical medicine and rehabilitation programs in developing countries (e.g., Ghana) and provides education enabling family-medicine physicians to deliver rehab-level care, with a particular focus on oncology populations. He previously led a Global Health and Disparities curriculum for house officers.

name: Melissa Tinney
focus_areas: Dr. Tinney is committed to medical education in Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation, teaching Fellows in Africa. Her areas of focus have been in limb loss/limb deficiency care and prosthetics and orthotics prescription and adaptive sports and exercise.

name: Sagar V. Parikh
focus_areas: Dr. Parikh’s work in global mental health is informed by the Grand Challenges in Global Mental Health initiative and focuses on improving treatments and expanding access to effective mental health care. He conducts effectiveness trials using multi-component strategies—medication, psychotherapy, biomarkers, and education—targeting patients, providers, and systems to address multiple determinants of health.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Latin America & Caribbean, North America, Europe

name: Srijan Sen
focus_areas: Dr. Sen’s research focuses on the interactions between genes and the environment and their effect on stress, anxiety, and depression. He has an ongoing collaboration through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute to explore the genomic and environmental drivers of depression among physicians in training in China and the US.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Heidi Burns
focus_areas: Dr. Burns specializes in child psychiatry and emergency psychiatry. Her research areas in global health have primarily focused on capacity building for subspecialties in psychiatry and suicide prevention.

name: Mohammad Ghaziuddin
focus_areas: Dr. Ghaziuddin's research interests focus on Autism/Asperger syndrome and related illnesses. He has helped establish specialty training programs in child psychiatry and mental health, most recently in Uzbekistan.
regions_derived: South Asia, Middle East & North Africa, Central Asia

name: Bruno Giordani
focus_areas: Dr. Giordani studies neurodevelopmental changes in cognition, behavior, and mobility associated with medical treatments and disease (including cerebral malaria and HIV/AIDS) and investigates driving distraction and performance across cultural settings.
countries_normalized: Uganda, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Nigeria, Ghana, Brazil

name: Shruti Jolly
focus_areas: Dr. Jolly focuses on improving the quality, access, and innovation of cancer care in resource-constrained settings through programmatic initiatives. Her clinical and academic interests include lung cancer and women’s health (including brachytherapy), with ongoing efforts to reduce treatment toxicities and improve equitable care pathways.

name: Shadi Azar
focus_areas: Dr. Azar has partnered with medical schools in Jordan to improve medical education standards and help develop special training for medical students and physicians on how to handle medical crises. Since 2003, Jordan has been the primary destination for refugees from nearby areas of the Middle East. Refugees traumatized by war experience specific stressors related to their status of residence. Together with limited access to health care, these constitute additional risk factors for developing somatic and psychological illnesses. Physicians require specific training for these populations.

name: Neeraj Chaudhary
focus_areas: Dr. Chaudhary's work explores mechanical thrombectomy for ischemic stroke in the developing world. World Stroke Organization has a mission to improve access to endovascular treatment of ischemic stroke around the world. He would like to be able to promote systems of care which will improve access for patients to mechanical thrombectomy for ischemic stroke.

name: Brian Fowlkes
focus_areas: Dr. Fowlkes’ research interest is in biomedical imaging, ultrasonics and image-guided therapy. His collaboration (link is external)through the Michigan Medicine-Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute seeks to validate a new ultrasound-based limb salvage strategy for critical limb ischemia using imaging biomarkers.

name: Prabesh Kanel
focus_areas: Dr. Kanel aims to improve the lives of people with parkinsonism worldwide—particularly in LMICs—by strengthening diagnosis, treatment, and care, and advancing research and innovation to prevent and better understand the disorder.

name: Amit Pandya
focus_areas: Dr. Pandya's work explores ultrasound applications in LMIC settings. Ultrasound has revolutionized the medicine for intra-abdominal processes. It involves no radiation and it is possible to take machine portably anywhere, provided there is electricity. It is also now available in laptop computer form, so can be run on battery.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Hemant Parmar
focus_areas: Dr. Parmar teaches neuroradiology basics and advanced concepts (including protocol building) to radiology trainees and staff radiologists, with prior teaching in India, Ghana, and Bosnia-Herzegovina and current training in Tanzania (since 2022).

name: Fay Pongmala
focus_areas: Dr. Pongmala is a research investigator at Functional neuroimaging, cognitive, and mobility laboratory with expertise in aging and neurological disorders especially Parkinson’s disease. Her research interests are physical activity, sedentary behavior, and mobility such as gait and balance. Dr.Pongmala has received Global REACH partnership grant and has an active collaboration with the faculties from well-known established universities in Thailand. She recently has started establishing the collaboration with the faculty from South Korea.

name: Mamadou Sanogo
focus_areas: To educate trainees in developing countries and promote access to adequate healthcare in underserved communities.

name: Xueding Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Xueding Wang focuses on medical imaging, ultrasound, and optics and is also interested in novel ultrasound- and laser-based treatment technologies.

name: Kevin Chung
professional_title: William C Grabb Distinguished University Professor of Surgery, Charles B de Nancrede Professor of Surgery
focus_areas: Dr. Chung leads the Taiwan Collaboration Platform through Global REACH, focusing primarily on partnerships with Chang Gung Memorial Hospital, Taiwan's largest healthcare system. His research focuses on capacity building in low-resource countries to develop a generation of committed physicians and surgeons caring for those in need, with an emphasis on children’s health.

name: Peter Ehrlich
focus_areas: Dr. Ehrlich focuses on international research collaborations and capacity strengthening. He works with global partners to study population aging and the consequences of visual and multisensory impairment on health and well-being in older adults and is invested in strengthening research capacity to improve health outcomes globally.

name: Robert Harris Gilman
focus_areas: Dr. Gilman specializes in pediatric reconstructive surgery and has developed remote pre-screening protocols to streamline patient selection. His team has operated on more than 1,400 children for conditions including cleft lip/palate and sequelae, congenital face/hand anomalies, trauma/disease reconstruction, and burn reconstruction.

name: Molly Hunter
focus_areas: Dr. Hunter is interested in burn prevention and improving outcomes for burn patients in resource-limited settings. She partners with burn centers in LMICs to aid in high quality research and implementation of setting-specific interventions for burn care.

name: Grace Kim
focus_areas: Dr. Kim is interested in the Intersection of global surgery and surgical education. She leads the ALL-SAFE laparoscopic surgery training project that aims to expand the use of minimally invasive surgical procedures in resource-limited settings.

name: Gifty Kwakye
focus_areas: Dr. Kwakye’s work focuses on strengthening colorectal surgical care and education in low-resource settings. She leads a multinational effort to establish Ghana’s first colorectal surgery fellowship, now training its third cohort of fellows. More recently, she has expanded her work to Rwanda, collaborating with local surgeons and educators to enhance rectal cancer management and provide targeted educational support for general and colorectal surgeons managing complex cases.

name: Yongqing Li
focus_areas: Dr. Yongqing Li’s research focuses on the early diagnosis and targeted treatment of sepsis and sepsis-induced organ dysfunction, particularly acute lung injury. His team has identified citrullinated histone H3 (CitH3) as a novel biomarker and therapeutic target in sepsis. In collaboration with Drs. Jianjie Ma and Katsuo Kurabayashi, Dr. Li has developed a humanized CitH3 monoclonal antibody (hCitH3-mAb) and integrated it into a rapid, bedside-compatible pre-equilibrium digital ELISA (PEdELISA) platform. This innovation enables the differentiation of sepsis from sterile inflammation within minutes using just 10 µL of blood. His research bridges mechanistic insights into peptidylarginine deiminase (PAD) enzyme–mediated NETosis with translational applications aimed at improving sepsis triage, monitoring, and patient outcomes.

name: Adeyiza Momoh
focus_areas: Dr. Momoh advances surgical-capacity building with a focus on microsurgery and breast reconstruction through hands-on clinical teaching. He also works on a multi-institutional project to culturally adapt a breast-reconstruction decision aid for sub-Saharan Africa, with plans for implementation.

name: Pauline Park
focus_areas: Dr. Park works with the AIIMS–U-M collaboration on trauma and ICU care delivery.

name: Robin Petroze
focus_areas: Dr. Petroze focuses on access to surgical care and surgical outcomes in LMICs. Her recent research focuses on neonatal surgical outcomes, hospital-acquired infections, and pediatric surgical oncology in low-resource settings. She also focuses on collaborative education development at the medical student and faculty levels.

name: Jeffrey Punch
focus_areas: Dr. Punch partners with colleagues in sub-Saharan Africa to develop sustainable kidney-transplant services, leading multidisciplinary teams (surgeons, nephrologists, nurses, social workers) who train and supervise virtually and on site. A prior similar effort in Ethiopia succeeded, and he is now planning work in Ghana to establish durable local transplant programs.

name: Krishnan Raghavendran
focus_areas: Dr. Raghavendran focuses on organizing and delivering trauma services and critical care in the developing world.

name: Gurpreet Rana
focus_areas: Ms. Rana provides leadership in identifying global information resources, cultivating partnerships across the health sciences community, exploring the role of global health informatics in instruction, integrating librarians into global health initiatives, identifying grant and funding opportunities, participating in global health research efforts, and collaborating with faculty to reduce health disparities through information resources.

name: Priyanka Gupta
focus_areas: Dr. Gupta is interested in developing sustainable collaborations between surgical departments to build programs that improve surgical education through technology and innovative learning tools. She has a particular interest in women’s pelvic health and surgical repair of fistula, incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse.

name: Evan Keller
professional_title: Richard and Susan Rogel Professor of Oncology
focus_areas: Dr. Keller performs spatial analysis of cancers. Current projects include evaluating the immune microenvironment in large B-cell lymphoma and planning for future needs/strategies to incorporate spatial analysis into precision medicine.

name: Ganesh Palapattu
focus_areas: Dr. Palapattu’s research examines human prostate cancer stem cells, the prostate tumor microenvironment, and mechanisms of prostate cancer progression. His collaboration through the Michigan Medicine–Peking University Health Science Center Joint Institute explores molecular drivers of recurrence and/or mortality following radical nephrectomy for renal cell carcinoma. As department chair, he also supports departmental efforts in Rwanda, Senegal, Denmark, Austria, and other locations.

name: Simpa Salami
focus_areas: Dr. Salami focuses on building sustainable surgical training and research programs through capacity-building and educational, clinical, and research collaborations in urologic oncology, with special interest in prostate and kidney cancer clinical care and translational research.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Mallory Klaunig
focus_areas: Dr. Klaunig's focus is on the identification and psychosocial treatment of people at risk for psychosis as well as those who have had a first episode. I completed my dissertation work in this area with the University of Stellenbosch in South Africa.

name: Zoe Zimmer
focus_areas: Dr. Zimmer is a Global Women's Health Fellow in the department of OB-GYN, interested in minimally invasive gynecologic surgery (MIGS) capacity building and training in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). Active partnerships in Ghana and Rwanda through the department of of OBGYN.

name: George Okoli
focus_areas: Dr. Okoli is interested in clinical epidemiology, pharmacoepidemiology, and health technology assessment, with a special interest in respiratory infectious diseases epidemiology, surveillance, and control, particularly, influenza, respiratory syncytial virus, and SARS-CoV-2 infections.
regions_derived: East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, Middle East & North Africa

name: Stephanie Kielb
focus_areas: Dr. Kielb been working in Rwanda for many years with a charity called the International Organization for Women and Development, where she currently sits on the medical advisory board. The organization runs three mission trips per year treating obstetric fistula and urogynecologic issues. She is working currently to build a urogynecology fellowship in Rwanda, as well as a urologic residency educational program with the Rwandan Urological Association.

name: Abdulsalam Soofi
focus_areas: Dr. Soofi's research focuses on diabetic kidney disease (DKD), a leading cause of end-stage renal failure. As diabetes rates rise worldwide, DKD has become a critical global health problem, contributing significantly to morbidity, mortality, and health care disparities.

name: Anand Singh
focus_areas: Dr. Singh's primary focus in global health centers on understanding and mitigating cancer therapy–induced cardiotoxicity, with the goal of developing strategies to protect cardiovascular health in patients undergoing cancer treatment. This work bridges oncology and cardiology to improve long-term patient outcomes worldwide.

name: Chenguang Wang
focus_areas: Dr. Wang's research focuses on uncovering new transcriptional programs in unspecified T-cell lymphoma in different geographical areas, as well as how tumor microenvironment may interact with lymphomas to confer resistance to conventional chemotherapy, which promote novel therapeutic strategies to improve outcomes for patients.

name: Eliza Tsou
focus_areas: Dr. Tsou has international collaborations in rheumatology and autoimmune disease research, with an emphasis on advancing scientific discovery and improving patient care through shared and complementary expertise.

name: Adrienne Lapidos
focus_areas: Dr. Lapidos focuses on the intersection of mental health and oral health; specifically, the oral health needs of people with mental illness and substance use disorders. She is an active member of the "Right to Smile Collaborative," an interdisciplinary and international group of researchers and clinicians in psychiatry, dentistry, and public health, who are working toward improved oral health outcomes, and reduced oral health disparities, among people with mental illnesses.

name: Sangeeta Khanna
focus_areas: Dr. Khanna is passionate about global ophthalmology education, especially neuro-ophthalmology education and collaborations that enrich our medical students and residents and provide value to the host country. She is a member of the Global Ophthalmology Consortium of the American Academy of ophthalmology, with a focus on education and global partnerships.
regions_derived: South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, Latin America & Caribbean

name: Mousumi Banerjee
professional_title: Anant M. Kshirsagar Collegiate Research Professor of Biostatistics
focus_areas: Dr. Banerjee’s global health research is focused on developing and applying rigorous and innovative biostatistical methodologies in the areas of cancer, maternal and child health, and cardiovascular diseases. Her teaching/training interests are in building biostatistical capacity in developing countries. Dr. Banerjee has mentored many undergraduate and master’s-level students at U-M on global health projects and internships and taught globally focused workshops/webinars/short courses In India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Cyprus, and South Africa. She also serves on multiple U-M and international committees and advisory boards related to global health.
regions_derived: South Asia, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific

name: Ana Montoya
focus_areas: Dr. Montoya’s clinical practice and research focus on improving the care of older adults in nursing homes and other settings, with a particular interest in dementia and health disparities. She is interested in partnerships to enhance medical education and improve the care of older adults in low-income communities.

name: Christopher Bichakjian
focus_areas: Dr. Bichakjian is the development of clinical and educational partnerships throughout the world to (1) establish and strengthen local dermatology training, (2) improve global dermatologic clinical care, and (3) provide opportunities for Michigan Medicine faculty and trainees to experience and appreciate global health care disparities.
regions_derived: Latin America & Caribbean, East / Southeast Asia & Pacific, South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa

name: Megan Killian
focus_areas: Dr. Killian’s research focuses on the understanding the mechanisms of tendon development, injury, and disease and how changes in tendon biology influence musculoskeletal health (including muscle and bone function). She is also interested in the genetic and environmental drivers of disease in musculoskeletal tissues.

name: Alejandra Arango
focus_areas: Dr. Arango's interest in global health is the development, cultural adaptation, and implementation of evidence-based mental health interventions in South America. She is particularly focused on youth suicide prevention and on meaningfully engaging families and communities in prevention and treatment efforts.

name: Maria Muzik
focus_areas: Dr. Muzik focuses on women’s mental health during pregnancy and postpartum, gender-based violence, child mental health, and multigenerational perspectives. Her interests in global health equity aligns around  themes of empowering women, strengthening systems for health, and technical solutions for health equity.

name: Nobuhiko Kamada
focus_areas: Dr. Kamada’s research focuses on the complex relationship between gut microbiota and gastrointestinal diseases such as inflammatory bowel disease and colorectal cancer. He holds an appointment with the University of Osaka’s Immunology Frontier Research Center.

name: Sarah Allexan
focus_areas: Dr. Allexan work involves improving medical Spanish proficiency among primary care physicians, as well as women's and children's health.

name: John Kao
focus_areas: Dr. Kao's research interests include gastrointestinal mucosal immunology, bacteria-host interactions, pathogenesis of immune-mediated gut inflammatory disorders (Helicobacter pylori, celiac disease, inflammatory bowel disease), and dendritic cell-based cancer vaccine.

name: Florian Schmitzberger
focus_areas: Dr. Schmitzberger has active collaborations with clinicians from Ukraine. Much of his research focuses on high-impact care initiatives such as blood transfusions and hemorrhage control, and he co-leads a Department of Defense-funded project aimed at developing clinical practice guidelines for the resuscitation of trauma patients.

name: Himanshu Patel
professional_title: Richard L Prager Research Professor in Cardiac Surgery and Director Frankel Cardiovascular Center
focus_areas: Dr. Patel leads a group that is working to develop a partnership with Kenyatta National Hospital, the largest public hospital in Kenya, to initiate an integrated cardiovascular service encompassing both adult and pediatric CV disease. As Kenya transitions its economy and society, its prevalence of CV disease in adults has increased. At the same time, it still retains the traditional burdens of congenital and rheumatic heart disease prevalent in developing economies. Dr. Patel’s multidisciplinary team is collaborating with KNH colleagues to understand their resource constraints and work to progressively help develop the vision, organizational and operational structures to enhance the rapidly growing population with CV disease.

name: Ginette V. Busschots
focus_areas: Dr. Busschots is interested in improving access to healthcare - particularly vaccinations, education, and primary care - in  Puerto Rico and the other associated Caribbean islands, including Vieques and Culebra.
